I couldn't find any information on the plant "Chronanthos orientalis." If you're referring to "Chrysanthemum orientale," here's a paragraph about it:

Chrysanthemum orientale, commonly known as Oriental Chrysanthemum, is a flowering plant native to Asia that has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The health benefits of Chrysanthemum orientale include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and volatile oils, such as borneol and camphor, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Chrysanthemum orientale is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, often in combination with other herbs, to promote relaxation and boost the immune system.
